entity: DB Master Finance Parent LLC and Subsidiaries

key_facts: Master Issuer Parent formed Jan 14, 2015 as special purpose Delaware LLC for securitization
Subsidiaries include Dunkin' Donuts Franchising LLC, DD IP Holder LLC, Baskin-Robbins Franchising LLC, BR IP Holder LLC, BR UK Franchising LLC, DB Real Estate Assets I & II LLC
Wholly owned subsidiary of DBGI (Dunkin' Brands Group, Inc.)
DBGI is indirect wholly owned subsidiary of IRB Holding Corp., ultimate parent Inspire Brands, Inc.
Securitization: assets pledged as collateral under base indenture; additional interest accrues if notes not repaid by anticipated repayment date

key_policies.fiscal_year: 52 or 53 weeks ending Sunday closest to Dec 31 (changed from Saturday in 2024)
key_policies.revenue_recognition: ASC 606 five-step model. Initial franchise fees allocated per restaurant and recognized over 20-year term from opening. Royalties recognized as earned. Renewal fees over renewal term. Transfer fees over remaining term. Incentives recognized over remaining term.
key_policies.property_depreciation: Straight-line: buildings 6-35 years, leasehold improvements shorter of useful life or lease term
key_policies.intangible_impairment: Annual review (first day Q4) plus trigger-based. Relief from royalty method (Level 3) for trademarks. Qualitative then quantitative test.
key_policies.income_taxes: All subsidiaries are disregarded entities for US tax — included in Inspire consolidated return. No tax sharing agreement. Only foreign withholding taxes recorded ($7.2M in 2024).
key_policies.lease_accounting: ASC 842. Operating and finance leases recognized. IBR based on Inspire's credit rating. Practical expedient: non-lease components not separated.
key_policies.advertising_expenses: Expenses match advertising revenues (pass-through). Production costs expensed on first airing.
key_policies.debt_issuance_costs: Deducted from debt carrying amount, amortized as interest over debt term using effective interest method

key_facts: Deferred revenue (contract liabilities): $168.9M (2024), $135.0M (2023), $98.5M (2022) — growing rapidly
Revenue recognized from prior-year deferred fees: $12.2M (2024), $11.5M (2023), $8.9M (2022)
Future fee recognition schedule: $20.8M (2025), $11.2M (2026), $12.7M (2027), $14.3M (2028), $15.9M (2029), $94.0M thereafter = $168.9M total
Accounts receivable: royalties/fees $51.6M, advertising contributions $31.1M, rent $4.5M, other $14.4M; allowance for credit losses $7.8M (up from $6.0M)
Property: land $56.1M, buildings $41.3M, leasehold improvements $69.8M, finance lease assets $11.7M; accumulated depreciation $43.5M
Other current liabilities: accrued interest $22.1M, gift card liability $0.4M, other $8.5M
Trademarks/trade names: $6.39B (indefinite-lived, not amortized) — 77% of total intangibles
Franchise rights: $1.95B gross, $1.67B net (29-year useful life)
License rights: $320M gross, $234M net (15-year useful life)
Favorable operating leases: $10.9M gross, $5.4M net
Total intangibles: $8.67B gross, $8.30B net
Annual amortization: ~$90M per year, $1.91B total remaining
Total securitization notes: $4.999B outstanding
Seven tranches with rates from 2.05% to 4.35% (fixed) plus two variable funding facilities
2017 Class A-2-II: $746M at 4.03%, due Nov 2027 (legal maturity Nov 2047)
2019 Class A-2-II: $379M at 4.02%, due May 2026

2021 Class A-2-II: $630.5M at 2.49%, due Nov 2028
2021 Class A-2-III: $1.164B at 2.79%, due Nov 2031
2019 Variable Funding: $150M at funding cost + 1.50%
2023 Variable Funding: $781.6M at funding cost + 1.75%
Fair value: $4.7B (2024), $4.2B (2023) — Level 2
Secured by substantially all assets of securitization entities
Remaining VFN capacity: $17M under 2023 facility
In compliance with all covenants as of Dec 29, 2024
Supply chain guarantees: $136.7M contingent liability (no amounts recorded)
Operating lease assets: $337.5M (2024), $351.9M (2023)

Operating lease liabilities: $325.2M total ($29.7M current + $295.4M long-term)
Finance lease liabilities: $10.5M total ($0.6M current + $9.9M long-term)
Weighted-average remaining lease term: operating 10.9 years, finance 13.4 years
Weighted-average discount rate: operating 5.4%, finance 5.0%
Operating lease cost: $59.0M (2024), variable $26.1M
Rental income (sublease): $134.4M, including $59.4M variable
Net lease income: $47.8M (Company earns more from subleases than it pays in lease costs)
Future lease payments: $442.2M total operating; future sublease receipts: $542.3M total
Assets leased to others (net): $126.6M
All subsidiaries treated as disregarded entities for US tax
No tax sharing agreement with Inspire
Only foreign withholding taxes: $7.2M (2024), $7.0M (2023), $5.9M (2022)
Statutory rate 21% but provision differs because disregarded entities included in Inspire return
Supply chain guarantees: $136.7M contingent liability as of Dec 29, 2024
Related to franchisee product purchase volumes over specified periods
No liabilities recorded — assessed quarterly based on internal forecasts
Latest supply chain guarantee expires in 2025
Legal matters: no material adverse effect expected on consolidated financial position
Management agreement with DBI: Company has no employees, DBI manages all franchise arrangements
Management fees: $113.5M (2024), $111.8M (2023), $102.7M (2022) — calculated weekly based on retail sales
Joint ventures: Japan and South Korea. Royalties from JVs: $7.4M (2024)
